UserProfile.title: User Profile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the application, defining their role and associations.

role.description: The user's role in the application.

adminId.description: If the user is a helper, this is the UID of their admin.

Nome.title: Nome
Nome.description: Representa uma pessoa na lista de nomes do usuário, incluindo detalhes de contato e status.

personId.description: O ID único da pessoa, geralmente do software de origem (ex: NW Scheduler PersonID).

phone.description: O telefone de contato da pessoa.

fieldGroup.description: O grupo de campo ao qual a pessoa está associada.

createdAt.description: O timestamp de quando a pessoa foi adicionada.

FieldGroup.title: Field Group
FieldGroup.description: Represents a group to categorize names.

createdAt.description: The date and time the group was created.

Invitation.title: Invitation
Invitation.description: Represents a pending invitation token for a user to become a helper.

adminId.description: The UID of the admin who created the invitation.

createdAt.description: The date and time the invitation was created.

claimed.description: Whether the invitation has been claimed.

claimedBy.description: The UID of the user who claimed the invitation.

claimedAt.description: The date and time the invitation was claimed.

definition.description: Stores the profile information for each user, including their role and admin association if they are a helper.
Each document represents an individual name in a user's list. The `userId` is the owner of the data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this list of names.

params.name: nomeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific name within the user's list.

definition.description: Represents a group created by a user to categorize their names. The `userId` is the owner of the group.
Stores single-use invitation tokens that link a new helper to an admin.

firestore.reasoning: The data structure now includes a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection to store user profiles with role-based access control information (`role`, `adminId`). Data collections like `nomes` and `fieldGroups` remain nested under `/users/{ownerId}` to maintain clear data ownership. A new root-level `invitations` collection manages the helper invitation workflow via shareable tokens. This structure allows for secure, delegated access using Firestore's path-based security and `get()` calls in rules to verify helper status.
